ocr.text: The evaluation of the therapeutic effects of various dosages on experimental animals is crucial for understanding their potential
applications in clinical settings.
Experimental Animals  Dosages mg/kg  % Increase 1 h  % Increase 2 h  % Increase 4 h
IC  No treatment  2.1 ± 0.47 a  6.2 ± 0.54 a  9.4 ± 1.76 a
A549  THP-1
Neutral Red  MTT  Neutral Red  MTT
3 hours  24 hours  3 hours  24 hours  3 hours  24 hours  3 hours  24 hours
The data presented in the table elucidates the impact of different dosages (mg/kg) on cell viability, as measured by both Neutral
Red and MTT assays, across multiple time intervals—specifically 1 hour, 2 hours, and 4 hours post-treatment. Notably, the %
Increase metrics across these time intervals provide insight into the dynamic response of the A549 and THP-1 cell lines to the
administered treatments. For instance, the percentage increase in viability after 2 hours demonstrates a marked difference
between the treatment groups and the no treatment control group (NL), highlighting the efficacy of specific dosages in
promoting cell viability. Additionally, the comparison of responses at varying time points allows for a comprehensive
assessment of the treatment's temporal effects, which is essential for optimizing therapeutic strategies. As such, this data serves
as a foundational reference for further investigations into dosage optimization and the biological mechanisms underlying the
observed increases in cell viability, ultimately informing future research and development efforts in this domain.
